text:
#### Consideration of revocation of a provisional order made by [ F38 the family court ] E+W


34.17. —(1) This rule applies where—


(Section 5(9) of the 1972 Act provides that [ F40 the family court ] may revoke a provisional order made by it, before the order has been confirmed in a reciprocating country, if it receives new evidence.)


(2) The court officer must serve on the person who applied for the provisional order (“ the applicant ”) a notice which must—


(3) If an applicant wishes to adduce further evidence—

#### Notification of variation or revocation of a maintenance order by the High Court or [ F41 the family court ] E+W


34.18. —(1) This rule applies where—


(2) The court officer must send a certified copy of the order of variation or revocation to the relevant court in the reciprocating country.


(Rule 34.22 provides for the transmission of documents to a court in a reciprocating country.)


Textual Amendments

metadata.title: Part 34 — Reciprocal Enforcement of Maintenance Orders
